Hexproof, indestructible

• Rewrite the Past — Return all creatures put into your graveyard this turn to the battlefield.

Curse of Death's Door.message: Fix five audit issues: Puzzle, Star, Throne, Corpse, Door

changes.old:
Enchant player
When Curse of Death's Door enters, enchanted player's life total becomes 0.
Enchanted player can't lose the game for having 0 or less life and can't gain life except from this curse.
At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, roll a d20:
• 1 | Two death counters.
• 2–9 | One death counter.
• 10–19 | One life counter.
• 20 | Gain 1 life and sacrifice Curse of Death's Door.
Whenever enchanted player is dealt damage, they get a death counter.
When enchanted player has three or more death counters, they lose the game.
When enchanted player has three or more life counters, they gain 1 life and sacrifice Curse of Death's Door.

The Marriage.message: Complexity audit + templating pass: simplify all card text

changes.old:
When The Marriage enters the battlefield, target player gains control of target nonland permanent you control, then you gain control of target nonland permanent they control. Put a blessing counter on each of those permanents.
Permanents with blessing counters have hexproof, vigilance, and "At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 1/1 white and green Spirit token with 'Whenever this creature attacks, put a blessing counter on another target permanent you control.'"
At the beginning of each end step, if you control four or more permanents with blessing counters, you may remove all blessing counters from permanents you control. If you do, search your library for any number of permanent cards with total mana value X or less, where X is twice the number of counters removed this way, and put them onto the battlefield. They enter with blessing counters on them.
changes.new:

The Pit.message: Color identity audit: 15 cards fixed + The Pit redesigned

changes.old:
When The Pit enters, put 60 depth counters on it. Roll 6d6 and lose that much life. The Pit deals 2 damage to each creature you control for every 10 depth counters on it. They each get a fallen counter until their controller's next turn.
Creatures with fallen counters:
• Can't attack unless their controller pays {3} per attacker.
• Have base power equal to half their original power, rounded down.
• Combat damage dealt to them is doubled.
Whenever a creature attacks you or enters under your control, The Pit deals 2 damage to it for every 10 depth counters on it and it gets a fallen counter until its controller's next turn.
At the beginning of your upkeep, remove 5 depth counters from The Pit. If it has no depth counters, sacrifice it.
changes.new:
Roll 3d6. Multiply the result by 3. You lose that much life.
Creatures you control without flying fall — The Pit deals damage to each of them equal to half the life you lost this way, rounded down. Creatures that die this way are exiled instead of going to a graveyard.
If this would reduce your life total to 0 or less, instead your life total becomes 1 and you skip your next two turns.

For each permanent sacrificed this way, its controller creates a 3/1 red Horror creature token with "When this creature leaves the battlefield, it deals 3 damage to you."

The Fiend.message: The Fiend: clarify wager loop and shuffle destination

changes.old:
Flash, flying
When The Fiend enters, starting with you in turn order, each player may wager. Each player who declines for the first time discards a card, loses 5 life, and mills three. Each player may wager only up to three times. Repeat this process until all players decline.
To wager, choose a mode not yet chosen by you this turn and flip a coin:
• Power — Win: Put six +1/+1 counters on each creature you control. Lose: Sacrifice six creatures.
• Knowledge — Win: Draw six cards. Lose: Mill six cards.
• Wealth — Win: Search your library for six lands, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle. Lose: Sacrifice six lands.
• Vengeance — Win: Target player sacrifices six permanents. Lose: You sacrifice six permanents.
• Immortality — Win: Gain 66 life. Lose: Your life total becomes 6.
• Dominion — Win: Gain control of six permanents target player controls. Lose: Target opponent gains control of six permanents you control.
When The Fiend becomes the target of a spell or ability, is dealt damage, or all players declined to wager this turn, shuffle it into its owner's library.
changes.new:
Flash, flying
When The Fiend enters, the wager begins. Starting with you in turn order, offer each player the chance to wager. The first time a player declines, they discard a card, lose 5 life, and mill three cards. Each player may wager up to three times total; after their third wager, they must decline without triggering the penalty. Continue offering wagers in turn order until every player has declined consecutively since the last wager was placed. Then the wager ends.
To wager, choose a mode not yet chosen by you this wager and flip a coin:
• Power — Win: Put six +1/+1 counters on each creature you control. Lose: Sacrifice six creatures.
• Knowledge — Win: Draw six cards. Lose: Mill six cards.
• Wealth — Win: Search your library for six lands, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle. Lose: Sacrifice six lands.
• Vengeance — Win: Target player sacrifices six permanents. Lose: You sacrifice six permanents.
• Immortality — Win: Gain 66 life. Lose: Your life total becomes 6.
• Dominion — Win: Gain control of six permanents target player controls. Lose: Target opponent gains control of six permanents you control.
When The Fiend becomes the target of a spell or ability, is dealt damage, or the wager ends, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.

Enchant player

Confusion — At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, that player exiles a card from their hand face down. They may look at and play that card as if it were in their hand.
Ineptitude — Whenever enchanted player casts a spell, if it wasn't cast from exile with The Puzzle, counter it.
Consolation — At the beginning of enchanted player's end step, they draw a card.

The Foreign Trader.message: Design review fixes + templating pass on remaining cards

changes.old:
Flash, Deathtouch
When The Foreign Trader enters, you may pay any amount of life in multiples of 5. For each 5 life paid this way, create three Treasure tokens, put an age counter on it, and scry 1. If you don't, sacrifice it and create a Treasure token.
At the beginning of your upkeep, for each age counter on The Foreign Trader, mill three cards.
Your maximum hand size is increased by the number of age counters on The Foreign Trader and spells you cast cost {1} less for each age counter on it.
Whenever you sacrifice a Treasure token, each opponent loses 1 life and you gain 1 life.

The Comet.message: The Comet: clarify Suspend overrides cast-immediately rule

changes.old:
Suspend 2 — {0}{U}{U} (When you draw The Comet from the Deck of Many Things, exile it with two time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter. When the last is removed, cast it without paying its mana cost.)
Impact — When you cast The Comet, you may choose target creature you control. If you do, all other creatures phase out until end of turn.
Until end of turn, the chosen creature gains flying, double strike, and "Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, draw two cards."
At the beginning of your end step, if a creature was chosen this way and that creature didn't deal combat damage to a player this turn, exile it.

Vengeful Archfiend.message: Vengeful Archfiend: airtight forcing + sticky nemesis marker

changes.old:
Suspend X — {0}{B}{R} (X is the result of rolling a d4. Exile this with that many time counters. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove one. When the last is removed, cast it.)
Vendetta — When Vengeful Archfiend enters, a random opponent gains control of it, and you become its nemesis.
Haste, trample, menace
Vengeful Archfiend can't block and attacks its nemesis each combat if able.
Torment — Whenever Vengeful Archfiend deals combat damage to its nemesis, put a rage counter on it. That player discards a card and sacrifices a permanent.
Vengeful Archfiend gets +2/+0 for each rage counter on it.
When Vengeful Archfiend leaves or its nemesis loses the game, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.

Divine Intervention.message: Divine Intervention: gate would-lose cast on intervention counters

changes.old:
When Divine Intervention enters, exile it. You may cast it from exile without paying its mana cost any time you could cast an instant. If you do, choose a mode you haven't chosen this game:
• Return all permanent cards from your graveyard to the battlefield.
• Your life total becomes your starting life total.
• Exile all nonland permanents your opponents control.
• Search your library for up to five cards and put them into your hand.
If you would lose the game while Divine Intervention is in exile, you may cast it instead. If you do, your life total becomes your starting life total, remove all poison counters from you, reset all commander damage dealt to you, and return target creature from your graveyard to the battlefield tapped.
When Divine Intervention is cast from exile, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.
Divine Intervention enters with two intervention counters on it. When you cast Divine Intervention from exile, remove an intervention counter. When the last is removed, exile Divine Intervention permanently.
changes.new:

• Search your library for up to five cards and put them into your hand.

If you do, your life total becomes your starting life total, remove all poison counters from you, reset all commander damage dealt to you, and return target creature from your graveyard to the battlefield tapped.
When Divine Intervention is cast from exile, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.

changes.old:
Enchant player, shroud

(You can't cast more than one spell each turn and can't activate more than one ability each turn. Artifacts, and enchantments you control lose all abilities except for Curse of Restraint. Spells you cast cost {W} more to cast for each bind counter on Curse of Restraint. Creatures you control can't attack unless you pay {2} for each creature attacking.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose X life, where X is the number of bind counters on Curse of Restraint.
Whenever a creature deals combat damage to you or a creature you control, it deals an additional 2 damage.
{0}, Pay X life and exile X cards from your hand or X nontoken permanents you control: Remove X bind counters from Curse of Restraint. X can't be greater than 2. Activate only as a sorcery.

changes.old:
Enchant player
Enchanted player plays with their hand revealed, skips their draw step, and can't cast spells during their main phases.
Mental Decay — At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, that player draws a card and exiles three cards from their hand face down. They may look at and play those cards as if they were in their hand.

changes.new:
Enchant player

Mindless — Enchanted player casts spells and activates abilities without choosing targets or modes. When a spell or ability that player controls would require a target or choice, choose it at random from the legal options.
Drooling — At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, that player mills two cards, then discards a card at random.

The Dragon.message: The Dragon: drop redundant "gain that color's abilities" on tokens

changes.old:
Flying, menace
The Dragon gains abilities based on colors of other Dragons you control:
• White: Vigilance, protection from black. Other Dragons get +2/+2 and vigilance.
• Blue: Ward {3}, hexproof. Other Dragons have ward {3} and can't be countered.
• Black: Deathtouch, lifelink. Other Dragons have deathtouch and "Whenever this deals damage, each opponent loses 1 life."
• Red: Double strike. Other Dragons have first strike and "When this attacks, it deals 2 damage to any target."
• Green: Trample, reach. Other Dragons get +3/+1 and trample.
Whenever The Dragon attacks, create two 3/3 Dragon tokens with flying of a random color. They gain that color's abilities.
Growth — {T}, Sacrifice a Dragon: Put three +1/+1 counters on The Dragon and draw a card.

The Tyrant.message: Design review fixes + templating pass on remaining cards

changes.old:
Vigilance, Menace, Indestructible
When The Tyrant enters, you become the monarch.
As long as you're the monarch:
• At the beginning of your upkeep, each opponent sacrifices a permanent. For each permanent sacrificed this way, create a Treasure token and put a +1/+1 counter on The Tyrant.
• Whenever you cast a spell, copy that spell. You may choose new targets for the copy.
• Other players can't cast spells during your turn, and your opponents can't attack you with fewer than three creatures.

changes.old:

• Cast a spell from your hand without paying its mana cost.
• Exile target nonland permanent.

When The Mystery enters, each player exiles the top three cards of their library face down.
At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player may exile the top card of their library face down. They may cast from exile one of those cards exiled with The Mystery this turn at random without paying its mana cost. If they do, they lose life equal to the number of cards they own in exile that were exiled with The Mystery.
When The Mystery leaves, shuffle all cards exiled with The Mystery into their owner's library.

The Demon.message: Design review fixes + templating pass on remaining cards

changes.old:
Flying, Haste
When The Demon enters, choose a player. That player becomes marked. (A marked player's maximum hand size is reduced by one for each doom counter on The Demon.)
Whenever The Demon deals combat damage to the marked player, put a doom counter on it and scry 1.
At the beginning of marked player's upkeep, The Demon deals damage to that player equal to the number of doom counters on it.
When The Demon has five or more doom counters on it, the marked player loses the game.

Temporal Stasis Sphere.message: The Donjon: fix stale emblem reference + anchor draw-skip cadence

changes.old:
Tick Tock — When Temporal Stasis Sphere enters, roll a d4 and add 3 to the result. Put that many time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter and mill three cards. When the last time counter is removed, exile Temporal Stasis Sphere at the beginning of the next end step.
You are dimensionally displaced. (A dimensionally displaced player can't be affected by any effect in the main game. They remain in the main game's turn order but can't interact with or be interacted with by other players. Spells you cast or permanents you play are exiled. Starting with your next turn, skip your draw step on even-numbered turns. Your maximum hand size is reduced by 2.)
When Temporal Stasis Sphere leaves, return and cast all cards exiled with it and The Donjon onto the battlefield at the beginning of your next upkeep. For each card returned this way, lose 1 life.

Haste, flying, shadow
Vengeful Revenant's power and toughness are each equal to 2 plus the number of creature cards in all graveyards.
When Vengeful Revenant enters, if no player is haunted by it, the player who drew it becomes haunted. That player gets an emblem with "At the beginning of your upkeep, sacrifice a creature. If you can't, you lose 1 life." An opponent chosen at random gains control of Vengeful Revenant.
Vengeful Revenant can't block and attacks the haunted player each combat if able.

Whenever a creature dies, put a Spotlight counter on The Theater.
{T}, Remove a Spotlight counter from The Theater: Choose one —
• Encore performance — Create two 2/2 white Human Performer tokens with "When this creature enters the battlefield, tap target creature an opponent controls."
• Inspire the audience — Each player draws a card, then you draw two additional cards.
• Standing ovation — Each player gains 1 life, then you gain 1 life for each creature you control.

The Campfire.message: Redesign 12 cards with D&D fidelity fixes and power scaling

changes.old:
At the beginning of your upkeep, put X Rest counters on The Campfire, where X is half the number of Rest counters on it, rounded up.
{T}, {W}: Put X Rest counters on The Campfire.
{T}, Remove any number of Rest counters: Choose one —
• Rejuvenate — Return target permanent card with mana value X or less from your graveyard to the battlefield, where X is the number of Rest counters removed this way.
• Invigorate — Put X +1/+1 counters divided as you choose among up to X target creatures, where X is the number of Rest counters removed this way.
• Recuperate — You gain X life and draw X cards, where X is the number of Rest counters removed this way. Skip your next turn.
changes.new:
When The Campfire enters, choose three —
• Return target permanent card from your graveyard to the battlefield.
• Put three +1/+1 counters on each creature you control.
• Draw four cards and gain 10 life.
• Remove all counters from target permanent.
• Create four 1/1 white Human creature tokens.
At the beginning of your upkeep, gain 2 life and scry 1.

changes.old: Legendary Artifact
changes.new: Legendary Enchantment

The Campfire.message: Add card database, gallery, tools, and project infrastructure

The Ship.message: Complexity audit + templating pass: simplify all card text

changes.old:
At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 2/2 blue, black, and red Human Pirate creature token with "When this creature enters the battlefield, if you control three or more Pirates, you may search your library for a land card, put it onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle."
Whenever a Pirate you control attacks, choose an ability from among first strike, deathtouch, lifelink, or vigilance. That creature gains the chosen ability until end of turn.
Whenever a Pirate you control deals combat damage to a player, draw a card and create a Treasure token.
Crew 8 (Tap any number of creatures you control with total power 8 or more: This Vehicle becomes an artifact creature until end of turn.)
Whenever The Ship becomes crewed, choose three abilities from: flying, trample, lifelink, double strike, menace, hexproof, or annihilator 1. It gains those abilities until end of turn.
changes.new:
At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 2/2 blue, black, and red Pirate token with "When this enters, if you control three or more Pirates, search your library for a land, put it onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle."

The Mute Hag.message: Redesign The Mute Hag: devastating sense deprivation curse

changes.old:
Hexproof
When The Mute Hag enters, target opponent gets a deprivation counter of a type chosen at random from among types they don't have. The types are:
• Sight — Can't look at cards in any zone except the battlefield.
• Hearing — Can't activate nonmana abilities.
• Speech — Can't cast spells during other players' turns.
At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, if that player has one or more deprivation counters, they lose 2 life for each unless they sacrifice that many permanents. Then they get a deprivation counter of a random type they don't already have.
{1}: Remove a deprivation counter from target player. Any player may activate this ability.
changes.new:
Shroud, indestructible
When The Mute Hag enters, each opponent gets a deprivation counter of a random type. The types are:
• Sight — Can't scry, surveil, or search libraries. Spells and abilities you control can't target opponents' permanents.
• Hearing — Can't cast spells or activate abilities during other players' turns.
• Speech — Creatures you control can't attack or block.
At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, if that player has fewer than three deprivation counters, they get one of a random type they don't already have.
Deprivation counters can't be removed except by an effect from a card named The Fates.

changes.old:
Hexproof
When The Mute Hag enters the battlefield, target opponent chooses sight, hearing, or speech at random from among the types they don't already have a deprivation counter for, then they receive a deprivation counter of the chosen type. (The types are sight, hearing, and speech.)
• A player with a sight counter is blind and can't look at cards in any zone except the battlefield.
• A player with a hearing counter is deaf and can't activate nonmana abilities.
• A player with a speech counter is mute and can't cast spells during other players' turns.
At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, if that player has one or more deprivation counters, they lose 2 life for each deprivation counter they have unless they sacrifice that many permanents. Then, that player gains a deprivation counter of a type chosen at random from among the types they don't already have.
{B}: Remove a deprivation counter. Any player may activate this ability.

The Giant.message: The Giant: explicit reverse-trample replacement + floor retemplate

changes.old:
When The Giant enters, you gain 20 life.
Each creature you control has base power and toughness at least 10/10, trample, reach, and "This creature can block up to three creatures each combat and deals excess combat damage to attacking player while blocking."
If a source an opponent controls would deal damage to you or permanents you control, prevent 1 of that damage.
changes.new:
When The Giant enters, you gain 20 life.
Each creature you control with base power less than 10 has base power 10. Each creature you control with base toughness less than 10 has base toughness 10.
Each creature you control has trample, reach, and "This creature can block up to three creatures each combat. If this creature is blocking and would deal combat damage to an attacking creature in excess of that creature's toughness, the excess damage is dealt to that creature's controller instead."
If a source an opponent controls would deal damage to you or permanents you control, prevent 1 of that damage.

Well of Minor Magics.message: Redesign 12 cards with D&D fidelity fixes and power scaling

changes.old:
{T}: Choose three different abilities from among the following. You can't choose the same ability twice. Activate this ability only as a sorcery.
• Add one mana of any color.
• Scry 1 or Surveil 1.
• Look at target player's hand.
• Target player mills a card.
• Target player gains 1 life.
• Tap or untap target creature.
• Target creature gets +1/+1 until end of turn.
• Target creature gains haste until end of turn.

changes.old:
Flying, Hexproof, Haste
{1}: Look at the top three cards of your library, then put them back in any order.

Sovereign Wish — Sacrifice The Winged Serpent: Choose one —
• Search your library for a card and put it onto the battlefield. If it's a creature, it gains haste.
• Exile all creatures and planeswalkers your opponents control.
• Gain control of all nonland permanents target opponent controls.
• Target player's life total becomes 1 or 40.
• Target player skips their next turn or you take an extra turn after this one.

• Remove the Deck of Many Things from the game and/or exile all cards from all graveyards, then each player shuffles their hand and all permanents they own into their library, then draws seven cards. You may put any number of permanent cards from your hand onto the battlefield.

The Book.message: Complexity audit + templating pass: simplify all card text

changes.old:
When The Book enters the battlefield, roll a d6 and add 2. The result is the number of experience counters you get.
{T}: Choose one —
• Enlighten — Exile a card from your hand: Put a page counter on The Book. For each page counter on The Book, you may play an additional land on each of your turns and draw an additional card during each of your draw steps.
• Comprehend — Look at the top X cards of your library, where X is the number of experience counters you have. You may cast one spell from among them without paying its mana cost if it shares a type with a card exiled with The Book. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.

Avatar of Death.message: Avatar of Death: "summoned" marking + remove protection from abilities

changes.old:
Flying, indestructible, menace, deathtouch, wither
Protection from abilities and colored spells
When Avatar of Death enters, mark the player who drew it. Each player's life total becomes 10. A random opponent gains control of Avatar of Death.
Avatar of Death attacks the marked player each combat if able and can't block.
When Avatar of Death dies or the marked player loses the game, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.
Whenever the marked player or Avatar of Death is targeted or dealt damage by an opponent, that opponent creates a token copy of Avatar of Death.
Creatures dealt lethal damage by Avatar of Death are exiled. When Avatar of Death deals combat damage to a player, that player loses the game.

The Juggernaut.message: The Juggernaut: restructure levels for compounding Constitution engine

changes.old:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
Creatures you control get +0/+X and ward X, where X is The Juggernaut's level.
Whenever one or more creatures you control are dealt damage and survive, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Constitution
Creatures you control have vigilance and enter with a +1/+1 counter on them.
Your opponents can't reduce your creatures' toughness or cause you to sacrifice permanents.
Level 3 — {3}: Constitution
Creatures you control have persist.
If one or more +1/+1 counters would be put on a creature you control, that many plus one +1/+1 counters are put on it instead.
Level 4 — {4}: Constitution
Creatures you control have indestructible. Each creature you control can block an additional creature, and a creature you control blocking alone can block a creature with menace.
Whenever a creature you control is dealt damage, put a +1/+1 counter on it.

Whenever a creature you control is dealt damage and survives, it deals that much damage to that source's controller.

Deck of Many Things.message: Rules Card: import D&D's once-per-drawer and deck-departs rules

changes.old:
The Deck of Many Things is a shared deck in the command zone.
DRAW AT YOUR OWN RISK!
Setup —
• Shuffle the Deck and place it in the command zone.
• Each player begins the game with 1 Fate Point (maximum 1).
Drawing —
Beginning on your fifth turn, once during your precombat main phase, you may spend a Fate Point to draw a card from the Deck.
Gaining Fate Points —
If you have no Fate Points, you may roll a d6 during your main phase. On a 6, you gain 1 Fate Point. If you already have one, you may draw a card from the Deck instead.
Resolution —
Cards drawn from the Deck are cast immediately without paying their mana cost. Drawing from the Deck can't be countered, doesn't use the stack, and can only be responded to by The Fates.
• Cards that would leave the battlefield are shuffled back into the Deck, except The Jester and The Fool, which are exiled.
• Resolve drawn cards one at a time in the order drawn.
• Emblems from the Deck can't be removed except by effects that specifically remove emblems.
• The legend rule doesn't apply to permanents from the Deck.
Optional Rules —
• Each player may draw one card from the Deck at the start of the game without spending a Fate Point.
• When drawing, you may declare 1 to 4 cards instead of one. Resolve each in order.
changes.new:
The Deck of Many Things is a shared deck in the command zone.
DRAW AT YOUR OWN RISK!
Setup —
• Shuffle the Deck and place it in the command zone.
• Each player begins the game with 1 Fate Point (maximum 1).
Drawing —
Beginning on your fifth turn, once during your precombat main phase, you may spend a Fate Point to draw a card from the Deck.
Gaining Fate Points —
If you have no Fate Points, you may roll a d6 during your main phase. On a 6, you gain 1 Fate Point. If you already have one, you may draw a card from the Deck instead.
Resolution —
Cards drawn from the Deck are cast immediately without paying their mana cost. Drawing from the Deck can't be countered, doesn't use the stack, and can only be responded to by The Fates.
• Cards that would leave the battlefield are shuffled back into the Deck, except The Jester and The Fool, which are exiled.
• Resolve drawn cards one at a time in the order drawn.
• Emblems from the Deck can't be removed except by effects that specifically remove emblems.
• The legend rule doesn't apply to permanents from the Deck.
Fate Glimpsed Once —
A player can't cast the same card from the Deck more than once per game. If you would reveal a card you've already cast from the Deck, shuffle it back and reveal another.
The Deck Vanishes —
Once every player has cast at least one card from the Deck, exile the Deck. No further cards can be drawn from it this game.
Optional Rules —
• Each player may draw one card from the Deck at the start of the game without spending a Fate Point.
• When drawing, you may declare 1 to 4 cards instead of one. Resolve each in order.
